channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized state party in swing state is leaving
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized the party in the presidential swing state announced Thursday he will be leaving his position in June after a six-year run that included a string of victories and “agonizingly close” losses.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Latest: Judge allows Trump administration to require everyone in the US illegally to register
item.description: A federal judge is allowing President Donald Trump’s administration to move forward with a requirement that everyone in the country illegally register with the federal government. The move will have far-reaching repercussions for immigrants across the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's tariffs threaten to end quarter-century era of cheap goods for U.S. consumers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s new tariffs threaten to push up prices on clothes, mobile phones, furniture and many other products in the coming months, possibly ending the era of cheap goods that Americans enjoyed for about a quarter-century before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopter crashes in New York City's Hudson River; sources say 6 dead
item.description: A helicopter has crashed into the Hudson River off Manhattan, the New York Police Department has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KREX 5

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's tariffs threaten to end quarter-century era of cheap goods for U.S. consumers
item.description: President Donald Trump’s new tariffs threaten to push up prices on clothes, mobile phones, furniture and many other products in the coming months.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Rugaber And Anne D'Innocenzio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Utah has nation's highest median household income when adjusted for cost of living, new report says
item.description: Utah has the highest median household income in the country when adjusted for differences in cost of living, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: The order applies to all regulations issued under laws governing things like energy appliance standards, mining and offshore drilling — as well as regulations issued under the Endangered Species Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ moves to drop charges against man it labeled MS-13 leader
item.description: The move to dismiss the charges prompted the unusual push from Villatoro Santos’s lawyer seeking to delay the dropping of any charges against his client.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens to put higher tariff rate back if countries don’t strike deals
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to reverse course on a 90-day pause on country-specific tariffs if countries don’t strike a deal with his administration before then.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Republican bill requiring proof of citizenship for voting passes US House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House passed one of Republicans' signature issues for the year on Thursday, approving legislation to require proof of U.S. citizenship when registering to vote for federal elections, one of President Donald Trump's top election-related priorities.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, CHRISTINE FERNANDO and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Author Jillian Lauren, wife of Weezer bassist, arrested after being shot by police
item.description: Jillian Lauren, author and wife of Weezer bassist Scott Shriner, was shot and injured by Los Angeles police and arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after pointing a gun at officers from her front yard, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jewish families channel Passover story after devastating losses in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: PASADENA, California (AP) — Aty Rotter lost her family home and her spiritual home in the ravenous fire that scorched a large swath of Los Angeles County earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House passes bill to stop 'rogue' judges from judicial overreach and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Brawl erupts in Kansas courtroom during murder sentencing
item.description: The murder victim's mother spoke to the judge before the sentencing. Her words sparked anger among some of the other people in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: Carina Branson, NewsNation

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo this week have been charged with participating in an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government last year, the U.S. Justice Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tips for spring gardening: Utah experts talk best times to prep and plant
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- Gardening can be many things to different people—sometimes a chore, other times a passion. If you're one of those passionate gardeners or someone looking to get started, here are some tips from experts at Millcreek Gardens to help you get your garden started.
item.dc:creator: Nate Larsen, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp to 78 cents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Postal Service is seeking a rate increase this summer that includes hiking the cost of a first-class stamp from 73 cents to 78 cents. The request was made Wednesday to the Postal Regulatory Commission, which must OK the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Babe Ruth's former Florida home goes up for sale for nearly $2M
item.description: The 1924 craftsman bungalow, located in St. Petersburg's Historic Old Northeast neighborhood, once belonged to Ruth, according to the real estate listing.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Ozempic and Wegovy: Sen. Fetterman pushes for Medicare to cover GLP-1s
item.description: Fetterman appealed to President Trump and Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. personally.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration appeals court decision ordering AP journalists back into White House events
item.description: The Trump administration said Wednesday it would appeal a federal court decision that ordered it to re-admit Associated Press journalists to White House events on First Amendment grounds.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dramatic video captures firefighter being pulled from rubble after home explosion
item.description: Dramatic body camera footage captured the moments an Illinois home exploded, trapping a firefighter underneath the rubble.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: At least 124 dead after roof collapse at Dominican club, officials scramble to ID victims
item.description: Rescue crews from Puerto Rico and Israel arrived Wednesday to help with the search. Officials said crews used sonar to detect what could be one person still breathing under the rubble.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A pipeline company filed hundreds of lawsuits against landowners. Now its project is threatened
item.description: MANSFIELD, S.D. (AP) — Jared Bossly was planting soybeans one spring night in 2023 on his 2,000-acre farm in South Dakota when he spotted a sheriff’s vehicle parked at the corner of his property. He had a hunch it wasn’t a social visit.
item.dc:creator: BY ERIC FERKENHOFF/LEE ENTERPRISES and JOSH KELETY/AP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kidnapping and conspiracy charges are dropped against college students in ‘Catch a Predator’ fad
item.description: A judge has dismissed conspiracy and kidnapping charges against five Massachusetts college students who were accused of plotting to lure a man to their campus through a dating app and then seizing him as part of a “Catch a Predator” trend on social media.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hip-hop exec, accused LA gang leader pleads not guilty to racketeering, extortion charges
item.description: A music executive who authorities allege is a leader of a notorious Los Angeles street gang appeared in court Tuesday to face a wide range of charges, including extortion, racketeering and conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Group calls for investigation into Ms. Rachel over posts about Gaza children
item.description: Rachel Griffin Accurso, who goes by Ms. Rachel to her more than 14 million subscribers on YouTube, makes videos for children.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rich on Tech Radio Recap: Nintendo News, Mesh Wi-Fi & Online Privacy
item.description: You see my segments on TV, but did you know I also host a national radio show called Rich On Tech? Each week, I share helpful tech news, tips, and answer your questions on radio stations across the country — from KFI AM 640 in Los Angeles to 710 WOR in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: YouTuber and entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson, called the tariffs a "nail in the coffin" for small businesses.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tariffs, including massive levies on China, go into effect
item.description: President Donald Trump's latest round of sweeping reciprocal tariffs went into effect early Wednesday, including a 104 percent import tax on China.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Roof collapse at Dominican club kills at least 113 as officials scramble to identify victims
item.description: The legendary Jet Set club in Santo Domingo was packed with musicians, professional athletes and government officials when dust began falling from the ceiling and into people’s drinks. Minutes later, the entire roof collapsed.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 96-year-old resident at southern Utah care home dies after being found in pool
item.description: A resident of a senior living facility in southern Utah has died after being found in the community's pool on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: International students at several Utah universities had their visas revoked
item.description: On Tuesday, a spokesperson for the University of Utah confirmed that about 17 international students or graduates have had their visas revoked as of April 8.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
